page: Vultr vhp-2c-2gb-amd is a High Performance family virtual machine featuring an AMD EPYC processor with an x86_64 architecture. It is configured with 2 shared vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with 2 threads, running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The hardware profile includes 2.0 GB of system memory, yielding 1.0 GB of RAM per core, and is bundled with 60 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. No GPU resources are included in this configuration.

Benchmark results indicate a divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is in the top tier (top 10%) at 3206.39, while multi-core performance falls into the average category at 6339.69. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance for large block reads (41.62 GB/sec) and writes (33.03 GB/sec), but weak performance for small block cached reads (280.84 GB/sec). Passmark CPU and memory scores are positioned in the bottom 25%, and database operations are poor, ranking in the bottom 10% with a score of 971.5.

Due to the shared CPU allocation and limited 2.0 GB memory capacity, this server exhibits resource constraints for memory-intensive or sustained multi-threaded workloads. However, the inclusion of fast NVMe storage and strong single-core burst performance provides qualitative cost efficiency for lightweight, non-continuous tasks. It is best suited for basic web serving, small-scale development environments, lightweight background workers, or low-traffic applications that do not require sustained multi-core execution or large memory footprints.
description: The Vultr vhp-2c-2gb-amd is an entry-level virtual server from the High Performance family, powered by an AMD EPYC x86_64 processor. It features 2 shared vCPUs (1 physical core, 2 threads) running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 2.0 GB of RAM and 60 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. While its stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranks in the top 10%, its multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics generally fall into the average to weak categories, with Passmark database operations scoring in the bottom 10%. Lacking a GPU, this configuration is qualitatively cost-effective for workloads with low memory requirements. It is best suited for lightweight applications, basic web serving, and development environments that benefit from fast local storage but do not demand sustained multi-threaded compute resources.
og_description: Vultr vhp-2c-2gb-amd is a High Performance virtual server featuring 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 60 GB of NVMe SSD storage, suitable for lightweight workloads and development.
meta_description: Vultr vhp-2c-2gb-amd is an AMD EPYC-powered virtual server with 2 shared vCPUs, 2.0 GB RAM, and 60 GB NVMe storage for entry-level workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly shared-CPU instance featuring AMD EPYC processing and fast NVMe storage for lightweight, single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC x86_64 processor with 2 shared vCPUs and 2.0 GB RAM
60 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage included
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Qualitatively cost-effective for low-memory, bursty workloads
Best suited for basic web serving, lightweight background tasks, and testing
